title: Aglet plugin manifest
description: Canonical shape of aplugin.json. See sdk/README.md for a tour. The Aglet runtime applies stricter validation at install time; this schema covers the structural fields editor tooling and CI can check ahead of build.

ui.description: Required by the host manifest validator. Plugins do not render UI, so this is a placeholder Page node.

id.description: Plugin identifier. Lowercase kebab-case, may start with a digit (e.g. 2fa, 2048, 3d-cube). No leading or trailing dash, no consecutive dashes. Note: `namespace` is stricter (must be a valid JS identifier).

name.description: Display name. Plain string, or i18n object keyed by locale.

version.description: Semantic version (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`, with optional pre-release / build metadata).

plugin.description: Marks this manifest as a plugin (vs. a regular aglet).

namespace.description: Action namespace exposed to the host as `<namespace>.<action>`. Conventionally matches `id`.

name.description: Action name. camelCase by convention; must be a valid JS identifier.

permission.description: Permission token required to invoke this action, of the form `<namespace>:<verb>`. The caller's aglet manifest must list it under `permissions`. Optional — actions with no `permission` are unrestricted.

kind.description: Plugin backend. `wasm` = sandboxed wasmtime instance + WASI imports allowlist (the common case for community plugins). `stdio` = subprocess speaking MCP-flavored JSON-RPC; runs with the user's full OS privileges narrowed by `capabilities[]` (see docs/STDIO_PLUGIN_SPEC.md). Static plugins live in the runtime repo and use `kind: "static"` there; that variant is not allowed in community plugins.

path.description: For `wasm` backends: path to the compiled `.wasm`, relative to aplugin.json. By convention `dist/<id>.wasm`. For `stdio` backends: path to the executable, by convention `dist/<id>` (per OS/arch under `dist/<id>-<os>-<arch>` once multi-arch packaging lands).

wasi_imports.description: Wasm backends only: allowlist of WASI / emscripten host stubs the plugin imports. Each entry is a fully-qualified `<module>.<name>` from the host's STUB_TABLE — review process gates additions. New plugins should declare exactly what they need so unintended imports fail loud at instantiate.

emits.description: Stdio backends only: resource URIs the plugin publishes via `notifications/resources/updated`. The host auto-`resources/subscribe`s to each URI on plugin load; arriving notifications are fanned out to aglets as `<namespace>.<uri>` events. URIs are plugin-defined (no closed enum) — keep them short kebab/dot tokens like `cpu`, `net.rx`.

capabilities.description: Stdio backends only: declarative allowlist of OS capabilities the plugin actually uses. Surfaced verbatim to the user in the install dialog (`This plugin needs: read /proc, no network, no exec`). Closed enum — adding capabilities requires a schema PR + REVIEW_PROCESS.md discussion. Wasm plugins ignore this field (their isolation is enforced by the sandbox + wasi_imports). Path-prefixed variants (`fs:read:/proc`) name specific dirs the plugin reads from; wildcard (`fs:read:any`) means the plugin touches arbitrary paths. v1: descriptive only — runtime sandbox enforcement (macOS sandbox-exec / linux bubblewrap) is future work.
